_publications._publicationTitle: Bioluminescent imaging of intracranial vestibular schwannoma xenografts in NOD/SCID mice.

_contextSnippet: the group of 8 stable or growing tumors had a bioluminescent emission greater than 100,000 photons/s (range, 142,478–3,106,300 photons/s; average, 618,740 photons/s) at 21 weeks
details: Mice with successful engraftment had bioluminescent emission greater than 100,000 photons/s (range 142,478-3,106,300 photons/s; average 618,740 photons/s) at 21 weeks, while non-engrafting mice had much lower intensities (range 0-76,010 photons/s; average 10,737 photons/s)
